Getting Started
# Getting Started[#](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/guide/getting_started.html#getting-started "Link to this heading")
_Want to follow along?[`Download this notebook.`](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/_downloads/414b23685416ae01f99bdfdb51ad9f77/getting_started.md)_
Performing a QEC experiment with Deltakit typically involves four steps.
  1. In the _circuit generation_ step, you generate a quantum circuit to implement the experiment of interest, such as quantum memory or stability. In this step, you compile a noisy physical circuit based on your QPU’s native gates and their characteristics.
  2. In the _simulation_ step, you simulate the circuit using [Stim](https://github.com/quantumlib/Stim).
  3. In the _decoding_ step, you choose a decoder to decode your measurement results. You can use both open-source decoders, like minimum weight perfect matching (MWPM), and propriety decoders, like Ambiguity Clustering (AC).
  4. In the _analysis_ step, you interpret the results of your QEC experiment, and calculate and visualise the logical error probability and error suppression factor Λ.


## Circuit Generation[#](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/guide/getting_started.html#circuit-generation "Link to this heading")
### Idealised Circuit[#](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/guide/getting_started.html#idealised-circuit "Link to this heading")
To generate the underlying circuit for quantum memory experiments, there are several common quantum error correction codes you can use, including:
  * Rotated planar codes ([`RotatedPlanarCode`](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/_build/generated/deltakit.explorer.codes.RotatedPlanarCode.html#deltakit.explorer.codes.RotatedPlanarCode "deltakit.explorer.codes.RotatedPlanarCode")),
  * Unrotated planar codes ([`UnrotatedPlanarCode`](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/_build/generated/deltakit.explorer.codes.UnrotatedPlanarCode.html#deltakit.explorer.codes.UnrotatedPlanarCode "deltakit.explorer.codes.UnrotatedPlanarCode")),
  * Unrotated toric codes ([`UnrotatedToricCode`](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/_build/generated/deltakit.explorer.codes.UnrotatedToricCode.html#deltakit.explorer.codes.UnrotatedToricCode "deltakit.explorer.codes.UnrotatedToricCode")),
  * Repetition codes ([`RepetitionCode`](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/_build/generated/deltakit.explorer.codes.RepetitionCode.html#deltakit.explorer.codes.RepetitionCode "deltakit.explorer.codes.RepetitionCode")),
  * Bivariate bicycle codes ([`BivariateBicycleCode`](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/_build/generated/deltakit.explorer.codes.BivariateBicycleCode.html#deltakit.explorer.codes.BivariateBicycleCode "deltakit.explorer.codes.BivariateBicycleCode")), and
  * (More generally) any CSS code ([`CSSCode`](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/_build/generated/deltakit.explorer.codes.CSSCode.html#deltakit.explorer.codes.CSSCode "deltakit.explorer.codes.CSSCode")).


To get the logical error probability with a quantum memory experiment, for example, you can use the rotated planar code to encode a logical qubit:

```
from deltakit.explorer import codes
# configure the rotated planar code
code = codes.RotatedPlanarCode(width=2, height=2)
code.draw_patch()
Copy code
```


```
(<Figure size 640x480 with 1 Axes>, <Axes: >)
Copy code
```

![../_images/81aca37be508d9fe4f4c0e60bc3d839517ac5e32c368016827167f97879d188b.png](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/_images/81aca37be508d9fe4f4c0e60bc3d839517ac5e32c368016827167f97879d188b.png)
You can then pass this `code` to the `css_code_memory_circuit` function to generate a noiseless circuit for a quantum memory experiment, for example, using a single round and measuring in the logical Pauli Z basis:

```
from deltakit.explorer.codes import css_code_memory_circuit
from deltakit.circuit.gates import PauliBasis

circuit = css_code_memory_circuit(code, num_rounds=1, logical_basis=PauliBasis.Z)
Copy code
```

This returns a `Circuit` object, which contains the quantum circuit that encodes a memory experiment using the error correcting code you chose in the first cell.

```
circuit
Copy code
```


```
Circuit([
    GateLayer([
        RZ(Qubit(Coord2D(1, 1)))
        RZ(Qubit(Coord2D(1, 3)))
        RZ(Qubit(Coord2D(3, 1)))
        RZ(Qubit(Coord2D(3, 3)))
        RX(Qubit(Coord2D(2, 0)))
        RX(Qubit(Coord2D(2, 2)))
        RX(Qubit(Coord2D(2, 4)))
    ])
    GateLayer([
        I(Qubit(Coord2D(1, 1)))
        I(Qubit(Coord2D(1, 3)))
        I(Qubit(Coord2D(3, 1)))
        I(Qubit(Coord2D(3, 3)))
    ])
    GateLayer([
        CZ(control=Qubit(Coord2D(2, 0)), target=Qubit(Coord2D(1, 1)))
        CX(control=Qubit(Coord2D(2, 2)), target=Qubit(Coord2D(1, 3)))
    ])
    GateLayer([
        CZ(control=Qubit(Coord2D(2, 0)), target=Qubit(Coord2D(3, 1)))
        CX(control=Qubit(Coord2D(2, 2)), target=Qubit(Coord2D(1, 1)))
    ])
    GateLayer([
        CX(control=Qubit(Coord2D(2, 2)), target=Qubit(Coord2D(3, 3)))
        CZ(control=Qubit(Coord2D(2, 4)), target=Qubit(Coord2D(1, 3)))
    ])
    GateLayer([
        CX(control=Qubit(Coord2D(2, 2)), target=Qubit(Coord2D(3, 1)))
        CZ(control=Qubit(Coord2D(2, 4)), target=Qubit(Coord2D(3, 3)))
    ])
    GateLayer([
        MX(Qubit(Coord2D(2, 0)), probability=0.0)
        MX(Qubit(Coord2D(2, 2)), probability=0.0)
        MX(Qubit(Coord2D(2, 4)), probability=0.0)
        MZ(Qubit(Coord2D(3, 1)), probability=0.0)
        MZ(Qubit(Coord2D(1, 1)), probability=0.0)
        MZ(Qubit(Coord2D(3, 3)), probability=0.0)
        MZ(Qubit(Coord2D(1, 3)), probability=0.0)
    ])
    Detector([MeasurementRecord(-7)], coordinate=Coordinate(np.int64(2), np.int64(0), 0))
    Detector([MeasurementRecord(-5)], coordinate=Coordinate(np.int64(2), np.int64(4), 0))
    ShiftCoordinates(Coordinate(0, 0, 1))
    Observable([MeasurementRecord(-3), MeasurementRecord(-1)], index=0)
    Detector([MeasurementRecord(-7), MeasurementRecord(-4), MeasurementRecord(-3)], coordinate=Coordinate(np.int64(2), np.int64(0), 0))
    Detector([MeasurementRecord(-5), MeasurementRecord(-1), MeasurementRecord(-2)], coordinate=Coordinate(np.int64(2), np.int64(4), 0))
    ShiftCoordinates(Coordinate(0, 0, 1))
], iterations=1)

```

To convert the `circuit` to [`stim`](https://github.com/quantumlib/Stim), you can run:

```
stim_circuit = circuit.as_stim_circuit()
Copy code
```

which is then easy to visualise:

```
stim_circuit.diagram(type="timeline-svg")
Copy code
```

![../_images/c10c29f00d49fc62d74e7bb01a17e284b0741fc236a67977fae48b6388a856fd.svg](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/_images/c10c29f00d49fc62d74e7bb01a17e284b0741fc236a67977fae48b6388a856fd.svg)
### Realistic Circuit[#](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/guide/getting_started.html#realistic-circuit "Link to this heading")
You can now add a noise model and compile the circuit into the native gate set of a realistic QPU.
#### Noise[#](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/guide/getting_started.html#noise "Link to this heading")
To simulate a realistic noise environment on a QPU, you can use several common noise models with [deltakit.explorer.qpu](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/api.html#api-deltakit-explorer-qpu), including:
  * Phenomenological noise ([`PhenomenologicalNoise`](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/_build/generated/deltakit.explorer.qpu.PhenomenologicalNoise.html#deltakit.explorer.qpu.PhenomenologicalNoise "deltakit.explorer.qpu.PhenomenologicalNoise")), which uses a fixed probability of depolarisation after each gate;
  * Standard depolarising noise ([`SD6Noise`](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/_build/generated/deltakit.explorer.qpu.SD6Noise.html#deltakit.explorer.qpu.SD6Noise "deltakit.explorer.qpu.SD6Noise")), which has a single probability of incorrect measurement and depolarisation after each gate, idle cycle, and reset operation;
  * “Toy” noise ([`ToyNoise`](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/_build/generated/deltakit.explorer.qpu.ToyNoise.html#deltakit.explorer.qpu.ToyNoise "deltakit.explorer.qpu.ToyNoise")), which parametrizes the probabilities of incorrect measurement and depolarisation after gate and idle cycle with two parameters; and
  * SI1000 noise ([`SI1000Noise`](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/_build/generated/deltakit.explorer.qpu.SI1000Noise.html#deltakit.explorer.qpu.SI1000Noise "deltakit.explorer.qpu.SI1000Noise")), a noise model inspired by superconducting qubits.


For example:

```
from deltakit.explorer.qpu import SI1000Noise
noise_model = SI1000Noise(p=1e-3)  # 0.001 physical error rate
Copy code
```

You can also configure more general noise models using [`NoiseParameters`](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/_build/generated/deltakit.explorer.qpu.NoiseParameters.html#deltakit.explorer.qpu.NoiseParameters "deltakit.explorer.qpu.NoiseParameters").
#### Native Gates[#](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/guide/getting_started.html#native-gates "Link to this heading")
With Deltakit, you can define a QPU’s native gate set by specifying its one qubit, two qubit, reset, and measurement gates.

```
from deltakit.explorer.qpu import NativeGateSet
from deltakit.circuit.gates import SQRT_X, SQRT_X_DAG, S_DAG, S, X, Z, CZ,RZ, MZ
native_gates = NativeGateSet(
    one_qubit_gates={SQRT_X, SQRT_X_DAG, S_DAG, S, X, Z},
    two_qubit_gates={CZ},
    reset_gates={RZ},
    measurement_gates={MZ},
)
Copy code
```

With the noise model and native gate set defined, you can now generate an object to represent the `QPU` object, and compile your circuit to simulate the target QPU.

```
from deltakit.explorer.qpu import QPU
qpu = QPU(qubits=code.qubits,
          native_gates_and_times=native_gates,
          noise_model=noise_model)
qpu_circuit = qpu.compile_and_add_noise_to_circuit(circuit)
qpu_circuit.as_stim_circuit().diagram(type="timeline-svg")
Copy code
```

![../_images/16b03c5b5e36fd86cebafe0c1b2f55fa9115ecff77e0076171935298bcd7bc92.svg](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/_images/16b03c5b5e36fd86cebafe0c1b2f55fa9115ecff77e0076171935298bcd7bc92.svg)
With the noisy, qpu-native circuit generated, you’re now ready to tackle the next step of your quantum error correction experiment: simulation.
## Simulation[#](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/guide/getting_started.html#simulation "Link to this heading")
To sample raw output from the circuit, you can use `stim` features directly:
  1. Convert to `stim` circuit
  2. Compile a `stim` “sampler” from the circuit
  3. Sample using the sampler



```
stim_circuit = qpu_circuit.as_stim_circuit()  # 1
sampler = stim_circuit.compile_sampler()  # 2
sampler.sample(shots=3)  # 3
Copy code
```


```
array([[ True, False,  True, False, False, False, False],
       [ True,  True,  True,  True,  True,  True,  True],
       [ True,  True,  True,  True,  True,  True,  True]])
Copy code
```

The output is a boolean NumPy array representing the seven measurement outcomes for three separate shots.
Typically, the focus is not on the individual measurement outcomes but on logical errors. Assessing those is the job of the decoder.
## Decoding[#](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/guide/getting_started.html#decoding "Link to this heading")
Deltakit currently exposes one decoder for use locally, [PyMatching](https://github.com/oscarhiggott/PyMatching). (However, there are many other decoder options when using the remote workflow, and there are many local decoder analysis and manipulation tools in [deltakit.decode](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/api.html#api-deltakit-decode).)
([`PyMatchingDecoder.construct_decoder_and_stim_circuit`](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/_build/generated/methods/deltakit.decode.PyMatchingDecoder.construct_decoder_and_stim_circuit.html#deltakit.decode.PyMatchingDecoder.construct_decoder_and_stim_circuit "deltakit.decode.PyMatchingDecoder.construct_decoder_and_stim_circuit")) is a helper factor that accepts a circuit and returns two things:
  * an object representing a Minimum Weight Perfect Matching decoder and
  * a modified version of the original circuit configured to operate with the decoder.



```
from deltakit.decode import PyMatchingDecoder
decoder, decoder_circuit = PyMatchingDecoder.construct_decoder_and_stim_circuit(qpu_circuit)
Copy code
```

([`run_decoding_on_circuit`](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/_build/generated/deltakit.decode.analysis.run_decoding_on_circuit.html#deltakit.decode.analysis.run_decoding_on_circuit "deltakit.decode.analysis.run_decoding_on_circuit")) accepts these objects and the maximum number of shots to sample, returning a dictionary that contains the actual number of shots and the number of failures (shots for which the decoder incorrectly assessed whether the observable had flipped or not).

```
from deltakit.decode.analysis import run_decoding_on_circuit
results = run_decoding_on_circuit(decoder_circuit, 1000, decoder)
print(f"There were {results['fails']} failures out of {results['shots']} shots.")
Copy code
```

To scale beyond simple experiments, it is useful to define the concept of a “Decoder Manager”, which represents the combined experiment circuit and decoder system. The [`StimDecoderManager`](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/_build/generated/deltakit.decode.analysis.StimDecoderManager.html#deltakit.decode.analysis.StimDecoderManager "deltakit.decode.analysis.StimDecoderManager") accepts the circuit and decoder objects and returns a decoder manager. Similar to the `run_decoding_on_circuit` function, its [`run_batch_shots`](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/_build/generated/methods/deltakit.decode.analysis.StimDecoderManager.run_batch_shots.html#deltakit.decode.analysis.StimDecoderManager.run_batch_shots "deltakit.decode.analysis.StimDecoderManager.run_batch_shots") method returning the total number of shots and the number of failures.

```
from deltakit.decode.analysis import StimDecoderManager
decoder_manager = StimDecoderManager(decoder_circuit, decoder)
n_shots, n_fails = decoder_manager.run_batch_shots(1000)
print(f"There were {n_fails} failures out of {n_shots} shots.")
Copy code
```


```
There were 11 failures out of 1000 shots.
Copy code
```

## Analysis[#](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/guide/getting_started.html#analysis "Link to this heading")
Deltakit also provides tools for summarising the results of experiments. To calculate the LEP (Logical Error Probability) and its standard error, use the [`calculate_lep_and_lep_stddev`](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/_build/generated/deltakit.explorer.analysis.calculate_lep_and_lep_stddev.html#deltakit.explorer.analysis.calculate_lep_and_lep_stddev "deltakit.explorer.analysis.calculate_lep_and_lep_stddev") function.

```
from deltakit.explorer.analysis import calculate_lep_and_lep_stddev
lep, lep_stddev = calculate_lep_and_lep_stddev(
    fails=[n_fails],
    shots=[n_shots],
)
print(f"LEP: {lep[0]}, LEP std: {lep_stddev[0]}")

# confirm the calculation manually
import numpy as np
from scipy import stats
lep0 = n_fails / n_shots
lep_stddev0 = stats.sem([1]*n_fails + [0]*(n_shots - n_fails), ddof=0)
np.testing.assert_allclose(lep, lep0)
np.testing.assert_allclose(lep_stddev, lep_stddev0)
Copy code
```


```
LEP: 0.011, LEP std: 0.003298332912245215
Copy code
```

Now you can start varying different parameters to see how that changes the logical error probability, such as the code distance. You can pass a list of decoder managers and number of shots to [`RunAllAnalysisEngine`](https://deltakit-docs.riverlane.com/en/stable/_build/generated/deltakit.decode.analysis.RunAllAnalysisEngine.html#deltakit.decode.analysis.RunAllAnalysisEngine "deltakit.decode.analysis.RunAllAnalysisEngine"), and use the `run` method to run your simulations. This method then returns a dataframe that summarises the results.

```
from deltakit.decode.analysis import RunAllAnalysisEngine

distances = [5, 7, 9]
all_shots, all_fails = [], []
decoder_managers = []
for d in distances:
    code = codes.RotatedPlanarCode(width=d, height=d)
    circuit = css_code_memory_circuit(code, num_rounds=d, logical_basis=PauliBasis.Z)
    qpu = QPU(qubits=code.qubits, native_gates_and_times=native_gates, noise_model=noise_model)
    qpu_circuit = qpu.compile_and_add_noise_to_circuit(circuit)
    decoder, decoder_circuit = PyMatchingDecoder.construct_decoder_and_stim_circuit(qpu_circuit)
    decoder_managers.append(StimDecoderManager(decoder_circuit, decoder))

engine = RunAllAnalysisEngine("my experiment", decoder_managers=decoder_managers, max_shots=100000)
df = engine.run()
df['distance'] = distances
df
```

<section class="tex2jax_ignore mathjax_ignore" id="getting-started">
<h1>Getting Started<a class="headerlink" href="#getting-started" title="Link to this heading"><span>#</span></a></h1>
<p><em>Want to follow along? <a class="reference download internal" download="" href="../_downloads/414b23685416ae01f99bdfdb51ad9f77/getting_started.md"><code class="xref download docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">Download</span> <span class="pre">this</span> <span class="pre">notebook.</span></code></a></em></p>
<p>Performing a QEC experiment with Deltakit typically involves four steps.</p>
<ol class="arabic simple">
<li><p>In the <em>circuit generation</em> step, you generate a quantum circuit to implement the experiment
of interest, such as quantum memory or stability. In this step, you compile a noisy physical
circuit based on your QPU’s native gates and their characteristics.</p></li>
<li><p>In the <em>simulation</em> step, you simulate the circuit using <a class="reference external" href="https://github.com/quantumlib/Stim">Stim</a>.</p></li>
<li><p>In the <em>decoding</em> step, you choose a decoder to decode your measurement results. You can use
both open-source decoders, like minimum weight perfect matching (MWPM), and propriety
decoders, like Ambiguity Clustering (AC).</p></li>
<li><p>In the <em>analysis</em> step, you interpret the results of your QEC experiment, and calculate and visualise
the logical error probability and error suppression factor <span class="math notranslate nohighlight"><mjx-container class="MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax="CHTML" overflow="overflow" tabindex="0" ctxtmenu_counter="0" style="font-size: 127.3%;"><mjx-math data-latex="\Lambda" data-semantic-structure="0" class="NCM-N"><mjx-mi data-latex="\Lambda" data-semantic-type="identifier" data-semantic-role="greekletter" data-semantic-font="normal" data-semantic-annotation="clearspeak:simple;nemeth:number;depth:1" data-semantic-id="0" data-semantic-attributes="latex:\Lambda" data-semantic-level-number="0" data-speech-node="true"><mjx-c class="mjx-c39B">Λ</mjx-c></mjx-mi></mjx-math></mjx-container></span>.</p></li>
</ol>
<section id="circuit-generation">
<h2>Circuit Generation<a class="headerlink" href="#circuit-generation" title="Link to this heading" x-intersect.margin.0%.0%.-70%.0%="activeSection = '#circuit-generation'"><span>#</span></a></h2>
<section id="idealised-circuit">
<h3>Idealised Circuit<a class="headerlink" href="#idealised-circuit" title="Link to this heading" x-intersect.margin.0%.0%.-70%.0%="activeSection = '#idealised-circuit'"><span>#</span></a></h3>
<p>To generate the underlying circuit for quantum memory experiments, there are several common quantum error correction codes you can use, including:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p>Rotated planar codes (<a class="reference internal" href="../_build/generated/deltakit.explorer.codes.RotatedPlanarCode.html#deltakit.explorer.codes.RotatedPlanarCode" title="deltakit.explorer.codes.RotatedPlanarCode"><code class="xref py py-class docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">RotatedPlanarCode</span></code></a>),</p></li>
<li><p>Unrotated planar codes (<a class="reference internal" href="../_build/generated/deltakit.explorer.codes.UnrotatedPlanarCode.html#deltakit.explorer.codes.UnrotatedPlanarCode" title="deltakit.explorer.codes.UnrotatedPlanarCode"><code class="xref py py-class docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">UnrotatedPlanarCode</span></code></a>),</p></li>
<li><p>Unrotated toric codes (<a class="reference internal" href="../_build/generated/deltakit.explorer.codes.UnrotatedToricCode.html#deltakit.explorer.codes.UnrotatedToricCode" title="deltakit.explorer.codes.UnrotatedToricCode"><code class="xref py py-class docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">UnrotatedToricCode</span></code></a>),</p></li>
<li><p>Repetition codes (<a class="reference internal" href="../_build/generated/deltakit.explorer.codes.RepetitionCode.html#deltakit.explorer.codes.RepetitionCode" title="deltakit.explorer.codes.RepetitionCode"><code class="xref py py-class docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">RepetitionCode</span></code></a>),</p></li>
<li><p>Bivariate bicycle codes (<a class="reference internal" href="../_build/generated/deltakit.explorer.codes.BivariateBicycleCode.html#deltakit.explorer.codes.BivariateBicycleCode" title="deltakit.explorer.codes.BivariateBicycleCode"><code class="xref py py-class docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">BivariateBicycleCode</span></code></a>), and</p></li>
<li><p>(More generally) any CSS code (<a class="reference internal" href="../_build/generated/deltakit.explorer.codes.CSSCode.html#deltakit.explorer.codes.CSSCode" title="deltakit.explorer.codes.CSSCode"><code class="xref py py-class docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">CSSCode</span></code></a>).</p></li>
</ul>
<p>To get the logical error probability with a quantum memory experiment, for example, you can use the rotated planar code to encode a logical qubit:</p>
<div class="cell docutils container">
<div class="cell_input docutils container">
<div class="highlight-ipython3 notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><code><span id="line-1"><span class="kn">from</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">deltakit.explorer</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="n">codes</span>
</span><span id="line-2"><span class="c1"># configure the rotated planar code</span>
</span><span id="line-3"><span class="n">code</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">codes</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">RotatedPlanarCode</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">width</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mi">2</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">height</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mi">2</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span><span id="line-4"><span class="n">code</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">draw_patch</span><span class="p">()</span>
</span></code><button class="copy z-20 inline-flex h-6 w-6 items-center justify-center rounded-md border border-border bg-background opacity-0 focus:opacity-100 text-sm font-medium transition-all hover:bg-muted absolute right-4 top-4 tooltipped tooltipped-n" data-tooltip="Copy code" type="button"><span class="sr-only">Copy code</span><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-100 absolute"><path d="M200 976q-33 0-56.5-23.5T120 896V376q0-17 11.5-28.5T160 336q17 0 28.5 11.5T200 376v520h400q17 0 28.5 11.5T640 936q0 17-11.5 28.5T600 976H200Zm160-160q-33 0-56.5-23.5T280 736V256q0-33 23.5-56.5T360 176h360q33 0 56.5 23.5T800 256v480q0 33-23.5 56.5T720 816H360Zm0-80h360V256H360v480Zm0 0V256v480Z"></path></svg><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-success-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-0 absolute"><path d="M382 799q-8 0-15-2.5t-13-8.5L182 616q-11-11-10.5-28.5T183 559q11-11 28-11t28 11l143 143 339-339q11-11 28.5-11t28.5 11q11 11 11 28.5T778 420L410 788q-6 6-13 8.5t-15 2.5Z"></path></svg></button></pre></div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="cell_output docutils container">
<div class="output text_plain highlight-myst-ansi notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><code><span id="line-1">(<Figure size 640x480 with 1 Axes>, <Axes: >)
</span></code><button class="copy z-20 inline-flex h-6 w-6 items-center justify-center rounded-md border border-border bg-background opacity-0 focus:opacity-100 text-sm font-medium transition-all hover:bg-muted absolute right-4 top-4 tooltipped tooltipped-n" data-tooltip="Copy code" type="button"><span class="sr-only">Copy code</span><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-100 absolute"><path d="M200 976q-33 0-56.5-23.5T120 896V376q0-17 11.5-28.5T160 336q17 0 28.5 11.5T200 376v520h400q17 0 28.5 11.5T640 936q0 17-11.5 28.5T600 976H200Zm160-160q-33 0-56.5-23.5T280 736V256q0-33 23.5-56.5T360 176h360q33 0 56.5 23.5T800 256v480q0 33-23.5 56.5T720 816H360Zm0-80h360V256H360v480Zm0 0V256v480Z"></path></svg><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-success-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-0 absolute"><path d="M382 799q-8 0-15-2.5t-13-8.5L182 616q-11-11-10.5-28.5T183 559q11-11 28-11t28 11l143 143 339-339q11-11 28.5-11t28.5 11q11 11 11 28.5T778 420L410 788q-6 6-13 8.5t-15 2.5Z"></path></svg></button></pre></div>
</div>
<img alt="../_images/81aca37be508d9fe4f4c0e60bc3d839517ac5e32c368016827167f97879d188b.png" src="../_images/81aca37be508d9fe4f4c0e60bc3d839517ac5e32c368016827167f97879d188b.png" width="315" height="485">
</div>
</div>
<p>You can then pass this <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">code</span></code> to the <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">css_code_memory_circuit</span></code> function to generate a noiseless circuit for a quantum memory experiment, for example, using a single round and measuring in the logical Pauli Z basis:</p>
<div class="cell docutils container">
<div class="cell_input docutils container">
<div class="highlight-ipython3 notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><code><span id="line-1"><span class="kn">from</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">deltakit.explorer.codes</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="n">css_code_memory_circuit</span>
</span><span id="line-2"><span class="kn">from</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">deltakit.circuit.gates</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="n">PauliBasis</span>
</span><span id="line-3">
</span><span id="line-4"><span class="n">circuit</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">css_code_memory_circuit</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">code</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">num_rounds</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">logical_basis</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="n">PauliBasis</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Z</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span></code><button class="copy z-20 inline-flex h-6 w-6 items-center justify-center rounded-md border border-border bg-background opacity-0 focus:opacity-100 text-sm font-medium transition-all hover:bg-muted absolute right-4 top-4 tooltipped tooltipped-n" data-tooltip="Copy code" type="button"><span class="sr-only">Copy code</span><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-100 absolute"><path d="M200 976q-33 0-56.5-23.5T120 896V376q0-17 11.5-28.5T160 336q17 0 28.5 11.5T200 376v520h400q17 0 28.5 11.5T640 936q0 17-11.5 28.5T600 976H200Zm160-160q-33 0-56.5-23.5T280 736V256q0-33 23.5-56.5T360 176h360q33 0 56.5 23.5T800 256v480q0 33-23.5 56.5T720 816H360Zm0-80h360V256H360v480Zm0 0V256v480Z"></path></svg><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-success-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-0 absolute"><path d="M382 799q-8 0-15-2.5t-13-8.5L182 616q-11-11-10.5-28.5T183 559q11-11 28-11t28 11l143 143 339-339q11-11 28.5-11t28.5 11q11 11 11 28.5T778 420L410 788q-6 6-13 8.5t-15 2.5Z"></path></svg></button></pre></div>

<p>To convert the <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">circuit</span></code> to <a class="reference external" href="https://github.com/quantumlib/Stim"><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">stim</span></code></a>, you can run:</p>

</span></code><button class="copy z-20 inline-flex h-6 w-6 items-center justify-center rounded-md border border-border bg-background opacity-0 focus:opacity-100 text-sm font-medium transition-all hover:bg-muted absolute right-4 top-4 tooltipped tooltipped-n" data-tooltip="Copy code" type="button"><span class="sr-only">Copy code</span><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-100 absolute"><path d="M200 976q-33 0-56.5-23.5T120 896V376q0-17 11.5-28.5T160 336q17 0 28.5 11.5T200 376v520h400q17 0 28.5 11.5T640 936q0 17-11.5 28.5T600 976H200Zm160-160q-33 0-56.5-23.5T280 736V256q0-33 23.5-56.5T360 176h360q33 0 56.5 23.5T800 256v480q0 33-23.5 56.5T720 816H360Zm0-80h360V256H360v480Zm0 0V256v480Z"></path></svg><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-success-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-0 absolute"><path d="M382 799q-8 0-15-2.5t-13-8.5L182 616q-11-11-10.5-28.5T183 559q11-11 28-11t28 11l143 143 339-339q11-11 28.5-11t28.5 11q11 11 11 28.5T778 420L410 788q-6 6-13 8.5t-15 2.5Z"></path></svg></button></pre></div>

</span></code><button class="copy z-20 inline-flex h-6 w-6 items-center justify-center rounded-md border border-border bg-background opacity-0 focus:opacity-100 text-sm font-medium transition-all hover:bg-muted absolute right-4 top-4 tooltipped tooltipped-n" data-tooltip="Copy code" type="button"><span class="sr-only">Copy code</span><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-100 absolute"><path d="M200 976q-33 0-56.5-23.5T120 896V376q0-17 11.5-28.5T160 336q17 0 28.5 11.5T200 376v520h400q17 0 28.5 11.5T640 936q0 17-11.5 28.5T600 976H200Zm160-160q-33 0-56.5-23.5T280 736V256q0-33 23.5-56.5T360 176h360q33 0 56.5 23.5T800 256v480q0 33-23.5 56.5T720 816H360Zm0-80h360V256H360v480Zm0 0V256v480Z"></path></svg><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-success-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-0 absolute"><path d="M382 799q-8 0-15-2.5t-13-8.5L182 616q-11-11-10.5-28.5T183 559q11-11 28-11t28 11l143 143 339-339q11-11 28.5-11t28.5 11q11 11 11 28.5T778 420L410 788q-6 6-13 8.5t-15 2.5Z"></path></svg></button></pre></div>

<section id="realistic-circuit">
<h3>Realistic Circuit<a class="headerlink" href="#realistic-circuit" title="Link to this heading" x-intersect.margin.0%.0%.-70%.0%="activeSection = '#realistic-circuit'"><span>#</span></a></h3>
<p>You can now add a noise model and compile the circuit into the native gate set of a realistic QPU.</p>
<section id="noise">
<h4>Noise<a class="headerlink" href="#noise" title="Link to this heading"><span>#</span></a></h4>
<p>To simulate a realistic noise environment on a QPU, you can use several common noise models with
<a class="reference internal" href="../api.html#api-deltakit-explorer-qpu"><span class="std std-ref">deltakit.explorer.qpu</span></a>, including:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p>Phenomenological noise (<a class="reference internal" href="../_build/generated/deltakit.explorer.qpu.PhenomenologicalNoise.html#deltakit.explorer.qpu.PhenomenologicalNoise" title="deltakit.explorer.qpu.PhenomenologicalNoise"><code class="xref py py-class docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">PhenomenologicalNoise</span></code></a>),
which uses a fixed probability of depolarisation after each gate;</p></li>
<li><p>Standard depolarising noise (<a class="reference internal" href="../_build/generated/deltakit.explorer.qpu.SD6Noise.html#deltakit.explorer.qpu.SD6Noise" title="deltakit.explorer.qpu.SD6Noise"><code class="xref py py-class docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">SD6Noise</span></code></a>), which
has a single probability of incorrect measurement and depolarisation after each gate, idle cycle,
and reset operation;</p></li>
<li><p>“Toy” noise (<a class="reference internal" href="../_build/generated/deltakit.explorer.qpu.ToyNoise.html#deltakit.explorer.qpu.ToyNoise" title="deltakit.explorer.qpu.ToyNoise"><code class="xref py py-class docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">ToyNoise</span></code></a>), which parametrizes
the probabilities of incorrect measurement and depolarisation after gate and idle cycle with two
parameters; and</p></li>
<li><p>SI1000 noise (<a class="reference internal" href="../_build/generated/deltakit.explorer.qpu.SI1000Noise.html#deltakit.explorer.qpu.SI1000Noise" title="deltakit.explorer.qpu.SI1000Noise"><code class="xref py py-class docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">SI1000Noise</span></code></a>), a noise model
inspired by superconducting qubits.</p></li>
</ul>
<p>For example:</p>
<div class="cell docutils container">
<div class="cell_input docutils container">
<div class="highlight-ipython3 notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><code><span id="line-1"><span class="kn">from</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">deltakit.explorer.qpu</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="n">SI1000Noise</span>
</span><span id="line-2"><span class="n">noise_model</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">SI1000Noise</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">p</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mf">1e-3</span><span class="p">)</span>  <span class="c1"># 0.001 physical error rate</span>
</span></code><button class="copy z-20 inline-flex h-6 w-6 items-center justify-center rounded-md border border-border bg-background opacity-0 focus:opacity-100 text-sm font-medium transition-all hover:bg-muted absolute right-4 top-4 tooltipped tooltipped-n" data-tooltip="Copy code" type="button"><span class="sr-only">Copy code</span><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-100 absolute"><path d="M200 976q-33 0-56.5-23.5T120 896V376q0-17 11.5-28.5T160 336q17 0 28.5 11.5T200 376v520h400q17 0 28.5 11.5T640 936q0 17-11.5 28.5T600 976H200Zm160-160q-33 0-56.5-23.5T280 736V256q0-33 23.5-56.5T360 176h360q33 0 56.5 23.5T800 256v480q0 33-23.5 56.5T720 816H360Zm0-80h360V256H360v480Zm0 0V256v480Z"></path></svg><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-success-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-0 absolute"><path d="M382 799q-8 0-15-2.5t-13-8.5L182 616q-11-11-10.5-28.5T183 559q11-11 28-11t28 11l143 143 339-339q11-11 28.5-11t28.5 11q11 11 11 28.5T778 420L410 788q-6 6-13 8.5t-15 2.5Z"></path></svg></button></pre></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<p>You can also configure more general noise models using <a class="reference internal" href="../_build/generated/deltakit.explorer.qpu.NoiseParameters.html#deltakit.explorer.qpu.NoiseParameters" title="deltakit.explorer.qpu.NoiseParameters"><code class="xref py py-class docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">NoiseParameters</span></code></a>.</p>
</section>
<section id="native-gates">
<h4>Native Gates<a class="headerlink" href="#native-gates" title="Link to this heading"><span>#</span></a></h4>
<p>With Deltakit, you can define a QPU’s native gate set by specifying its one qubit,
two qubit, reset, and measurement gates.</p>
<div class="cell docutils container">
<div class="cell_input docutils container">
<div class="highlight-ipython3 notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><code><span id="line-1"><span class="kn">from</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">deltakit.explorer.qpu</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="n">NativeGateSet</span>
</span><span id="line-2"><span class="kn">from</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">deltakit.circuit.gates</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="n">SQRT_X</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">SQRT_X_DAG</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">S_DAG</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">S</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">X</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">Z</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">CZ</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="n">RZ</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">MZ</span>
</span><span id="line-3"><span class="n">native_gates</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">NativeGateSet</span><span class="p">(</span>
</span><span id="line-4">    <span class="n">one_qubit_gates</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="p">{</span><span class="n">SQRT_X</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">SQRT_X_DAG</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">S_DAG</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">S</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">X</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">Z</span><span class="p">},</span>
</span><span id="line-5">    <span class="n">two_qubit_gates</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="p">{</span><span class="n">CZ</span><span class="p">},</span>
</span><span id="line-6">    <span class="n">reset_gates</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="p">{</span><span class="n">RZ</span><span class="p">},</span>
</span><span id="line-7">    <span class="n">measurement_gates</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="p">{</span><span class="n">MZ</span><span class="p">},</span>
</span><span id="line-8"><span class="p">)</span>
</span></code><button class="copy z-20 inline-flex h-6 w-6 items-center justify-center rounded-md border border-border bg-background opacity-0 focus:opacity-100 text-sm font-medium transition-all hover:bg-muted absolute right-4 top-4 tooltipped tooltipped-n" data-tooltip="Copy code" type="button"><span class="sr-only">Copy code</span><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-100 absolute"><path d="M200 976q-33 0-56.5-23.5T120 896V376q0-17 11.5-28.5T160 336q17 0 28.5 11.5T200 376v520h400q17 0 28.5 11.5T640 936q0 17-11.5 28.5T600 976H200Zm160-160q-33 0-56.5-23.5T280 736V256q0-33 23.5-56.5T360 176h360q33 0 56.5 23.5T800 256v480q0 33-23.5 56.5T720 816H360Zm0-80h360V256H360v480Zm0 0V256v480Z"></path></svg><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-success-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-0 absolute"><path d="M382 799q-8 0-15-2.5t-13-8.5L182 616q-11-11-10.5-28.5T183 559q11-11 28-11t28 11l143 143 339-339q11-11 28.5-11t28.5 11q11 11 11 28.5T778 420L410 788q-6 6-13 8.5t-15 2.5Z"></path></svg></button></pre></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<p>With the noise model and native gate set defined, you can now generate an object to represent the <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">QPU</span></code> object, and compile your circuit to simulate the target QPU.</p>
<div class="cell docutils container">
<div class="cell_input docutils container">
<div class="highlight-ipython3 notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><code><span id="line-1"><span class="kn">from</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">deltakit.explorer.qpu</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="n">QPU</span>
</span><span id="line-2"><span class="n">qpu</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">QPU</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">qubits</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="n">code</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">qubits</span><span class="p">,</span>
</span><span id="line-3">          <span class="n">native_gates_and_times</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="n">native_gates</span><span class="p">,</span>
</span><span id="line-4">          <span class="n">noise_model</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="n">noise_model</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span><span id="line-5"><span class="n">qpu_circuit</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">qpu</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">compile_and_add_noise_to_circuit</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">circuit</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span><span id="line-6"><span class="n">qpu_circuit</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">as_stim_circuit</span><span class="p">()</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">diagram</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nb">type</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s2">"timeline-svg"</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span></code><button class="copy z-20 inline-flex h-6 w-6 items-center justify-center rounded-md border border-border bg-background opacity-0 focus:opacity-100 text-sm font-medium transition-all hover:bg-muted absolute right-4 top-4 tooltipped tooltipped-n" data-tooltip="Copy code" type="button"><span class="sr-only">Copy code</span><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-100 absolute"><path d="M200 976q-33 0-56.5-23.5T120 896V376q0-17 11.5-28.5T160 336q17 0 28.5 11.5T200 376v520h400q17 0 28.5 11.5T640 936q0 17-11.5 28.5T600 976H200Zm160-160q-33 0-56.5-23.5T280 736V256q0-33 23.5-56.5T360 176h360q33 0 56.5 23.5T800 256v480q0 33-23.5 56.5T720 816H360Zm0-80h360V256H360v480Zm0 0V256v480Z"></path></svg><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-success-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-0 absolute"><path d="M382 799q-8 0-15-2.5t-13-8.5L182 616q-11-11-10.5-28.5T183 559q11-11 28-11t28 11l143 143 339-339q11-11 28.5-11t28.5 11q11 11 11 28.5T778 420L410 788q-6 6-13 8.5t-15 2.5Z"></path></svg></button></pre></div>

</div>
</div>
<p>With the noisy, qpu-native circuit generated, you’re now ready to tackle the next step of your quantum error correction experiment: simulation.</p>
</section>
</section>
</section>
<section id="simulation">
<h2>Simulation<a class="headerlink" href="#simulation" title="Link to this heading" x-intersect.margin.0%.0%.-70%.0%="activeSection = '#simulation'"><span>#</span></a></h2>
<p>To sample raw output from the circuit, you can use <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">stim</span></code> features directly:</p>
<ol class="arabic simple">
<li><p>Convert to <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">stim</span></code> circuit</p></li>
<li><p>Compile a <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">stim</span></code> “sampler” from the circuit</p></li>
<li><p>Sample using the sampler</p></li>
</ol>
<div class="cell docutils container">
<div class="cell_input docutils container">
<div class="highlight-ipython3 notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><code><span id="line-1"><span class="n">stim_circuit</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">qpu_circuit</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">as_stim_circuit</span><span class="p">()</span>  <span class="c1"># 1</span>
</span><span id="line-2"><span class="n">sampler</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">stim_circuit</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">compile_sampler</span><span class="p">()</span>  <span class="c1"># 2</span>
</span><span id="line-3"><span class="n">sampler</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">sample</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">shots</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mi">3</span><span class="p">)</span>  <span class="c1"># 3</span>
</span></code><button class="copy z-20 inline-flex h-6 w-6 items-center justify-center rounded-md border border-border bg-background opacity-0 focus:opacity-100 text-sm font-medium transition-all hover:bg-muted absolute right-4 top-4 tooltipped tooltipped-n" data-tooltip="Copy code" type="button"><span class="sr-only">Copy code</span><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-100 absolute"><path d="M200 976q-33 0-56.5-23.5T120 896V376q0-17 11.5-28.5T160 336q17 0 28.5 11.5T200 376v520h400q17 0 28.5 11.5T640 936q0 17-11.5 28.5T600 976H200Zm160-160q-33 0-56.5-23.5T280 736V256q0-33 23.5-56.5T360 176h360q33 0 56.5 23.5T800 256v480q0 33-23.5 56.5T720 816H360Zm0-80h360V256H360v480Zm0 0V256v480Z"></path></svg><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-success-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-0 absolute"><path d="M382 799q-8 0-15-2.5t-13-8.5L182 616q-11-11-10.5-28.5T183 559q11-11 28-11t28 11l143 143 339-339q11-11 28.5-11t28.5 11q11 11 11 28.5T778 420L410 788q-6 6-13 8.5t-15 2.5Z"></path></svg></button></pre></div>

</div>

</div>
</div>
</div>
<p>The output is a boolean NumPy array representing the seven measurement outcomes for three separate shots.</p>
<p>Typically, the focus is not on the individual measurement outcomes but on logical errors. Assessing those is the job of the decoder.</p>
</section>
<section id="decoding">
<h2>Decoding<a class="headerlink" href="#decoding" title="Link to this heading" x-intersect.margin.0%.0%.-70%.0%="activeSection = '#decoding'"><span>#</span></a></h2>
<p>Deltakit currently exposes one decoder for use locally, <a class="reference external" href="https://github.com/oscarhiggott/PyMatching">PyMatching</a>. (However, there are many other decoder options when using the remote workflow, and there are many local decoder analysis and manipulation tools in <a class="reference internal" href="../api.html#api-deltakit-decode"><span class="std std-ref">deltakit.decode</span></a>.)</p>
<p>(<a class="reference internal" href="../_build/generated/methods/deltakit.decode.PyMatchingDecoder.construct_decoder_and_stim_circuit.html#deltakit.decode.PyMatchingDecoder.construct_decoder_and_stim_circuit" title="deltakit.decode.PyMatchingDecoder.construct_decoder_and_stim_circuit"><code class="xref py py-meth docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">PyMatchingDecoder.construct_decoder_and_stim_circuit</span></code></a>) is a helper factor that accepts a circuit and returns two things:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p>an object representing a Minimum Weight Perfect Matching decoder and</p></li>
<li><p>a modified version of the original circuit configured to operate with the decoder.</p></li>
</ul>
<div class="cell docutils container">
<div class="cell_input docutils container">
<div class="highlight-ipython3 notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><code><span id="line-1"><span class="kn">from</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">deltakit.decode</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="n">PyMatchingDecoder</span>
</span><span id="line-2"><span class="n">decoder</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">decoder_circuit</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">PyMatchingDecoder</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">construct_decoder_and_stim_circuit</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">qpu_circuit</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span></code><button class="copy z-20 inline-flex h-6 w-6 items-center justify-center rounded-md border border-border bg-background opacity-0 focus:opacity-100 text-sm font-medium transition-all hover:bg-muted absolute right-4 top-4 tooltipped tooltipped-n" data-tooltip="Copy code" type="button"><span class="sr-only">Copy code</span><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-100 absolute"><path d="M200 976q-33 0-56.5-23.5T120 896V376q0-17 11.5-28.5T160 336q17 0 28.5 11.5T200 376v520h400q17 0 28.5 11.5T640 936q0 17-11.5 28.5T600 976H200Zm160-160q-33 0-56.5-23.5T280 736V256q0-33 23.5-56.5T360 176h360q33 0 56.5 23.5T800 256v480q0 33-23.5 56.5T720 816H360Zm0-80h360V256H360v480Zm0 0V256v480Z"></path></svg><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-success-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-0 absolute"><path d="M382 799q-8 0-15-2.5t-13-8.5L182 616q-11-11-10.5-28.5T183 559q11-11 28-11t28 11l143 143 339-339q11-11 28.5-11t28.5 11q11 11 11 28.5T778 420L410 788q-6 6-13 8.5t-15 2.5Z"></path></svg></button></pre></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<p>(<a class="reference internal" href="../_build/generated/deltakit.decode.analysis.run_decoding_on_circuit.html#deltakit.decode.analysis.run_decoding_on_circuit" title="deltakit.decode.analysis.run_decoding_on_circuit"><code class="xref py py-meth docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">run_decoding_on_circuit</span></code></a>) accepts these objects and the maximum number of shots to sample, returning a dictionary that contains the actual number of shots and the number of failures (shots for which the decoder incorrectly assessed whether the observable had flipped or not).</p>
<div class="cell docutils container">
<div class="cell_input docutils container">
<div class="highlight-ipython3 notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><code><span id="line-1"><span class="kn">from</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">deltakit.decode.analysis</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="n">run_decoding_on_circuit</span>
</span><span id="line-2"><span class="n">results</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">run_decoding_on_circuit</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">decoder_circuit</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="mi">1000</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">decoder</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span><span id="line-3"><span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="sa">f</span><span class="s2">"There were </span><span class="si">{</span><span class="n">results</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'fails'</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="si">}</span><span class="s2"> failures out of </span><span class="si">{</span><span class="n">results</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'shots'</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="si">}</span><span class="s2"> shots."</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span></code><button class="copy z-20 inline-flex h-6 w-6 items-center justify-center rounded-md border border-border bg-background opacity-0 focus:opacity-100 text-sm font-medium transition-all hover:bg-muted absolute right-4 top-4 tooltipped tooltipped-n" data-tooltip="Copy code" type="button"><span class="sr-only">Copy code</span><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-100 absolute"><path d="M200 976q-33 0-56.5-23.5T120 896V376q0-17 11.5-28.5T160 336q17 0 28.5 11.5T200 376v520h400q17 0 28.5 11.5T640 936q0 17-11.5 28.5T600 976H200Zm160-160q-33 0-56.5-23.5T280 736V256q0-33 23.5-56.5T360 176h360q33 0 56.5 23.5T800 256v480q0 33-23.5 56.5T720 816H360Zm0-80h360V256H360v480Zm0 0V256v480Z"></path></svg><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-success-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-0 absolute"><path d="M382 799q-8 0-15-2.5t-13-8.5L182 616q-11-11-10.5-28.5T183 559q11-11 28-11t28 11l143 143 339-339q11-11 28.5-11t28.5 11q11 11 11 28.5T778 420L410 788q-6 6-13 8.5t-15 2.5Z"></path></svg></button></pre></div>

</div>
<p>To scale beyond simple experiments, it is useful to define the concept of a “Decoder Manager”, which represents the combined experiment circuit and decoder system. The <a class="reference internal" href="../_build/generated/deltakit.decode.analysis.StimDecoderManager.html#deltakit.decode.analysis.StimDecoderManager" title="deltakit.decode.analysis.StimDecoderManager"><code class="xref py py-class docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">StimDecoderManager</span></code></a> accepts the circuit and decoder objects and returns a decoder manager. Similar to the <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">run_decoding_on_circuit</span></code> function, its <a class="reference internal" href="../_build/generated/methods/deltakit.decode.analysis.StimDecoderManager.run_batch_shots.html#deltakit.decode.analysis.StimDecoderManager.run_batch_shots" title="deltakit.decode.analysis.StimDecoderManager.run_batch_shots"><code class="xref py py-class docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">run_batch_shots</span></code></a> method returning the total number of shots and the number of failures.</p>
<div class="cell docutils container">
<div class="cell_input docutils container">
<div class="highlight-ipython3 notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><code><span id="line-1"><span class="kn">from</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">deltakit.decode.analysis</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="n">StimDecoderManager</span>
</span><span id="line-2"><span class="n">decoder_manager</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">StimDecoderManager</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">decoder_circuit</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">decoder</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span><span id="line-3"><span class="n">n_shots</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">n_fails</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">decoder_manager</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">run_batch_shots</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="mi">1000</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span><span id="line-4"><span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="sa">f</span><span class="s2">"There were </span><span class="si">{</span><span class="n">n_fails</span><span class="si">}</span><span class="s2"> failures out of </span><span class="si">{</span><span class="n">n_shots</span><span class="si">}</span><span class="s2"> shots."</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span></code><button class="copy z-20 inline-flex h-6 w-6 items-center justify-center rounded-md border border-border bg-background opacity-0 focus:opacity-100 text-sm font-medium transition-all hover:bg-muted absolute right-4 top-4 tooltipped tooltipped-n" data-tooltip="Copy code" type="button"><span class="sr-only">Copy code</span><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-100 absolute"><path d="M200 976q-33 0-56.5-23.5T120 896V376q0-17 11.5-28.5T160 336q17 0 28.5 11.5T200 376v520h400q17 0 28.5 11.5T640 936q0 17-11.5 28.5T600 976H200Zm160-160q-33 0-56.5-23.5T280 736V256q0-33 23.5-56.5T360 176h360q33 0 56.5 23.5T800 256v480q0 33-23.5 56.5T720 816H360Zm0-80h360V256H360v480Zm0 0V256v480Z"></path></svg><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-success-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-0 absolute"><path d="M382 799q-8 0-15-2.5t-13-8.5L182 616q-11-11-10.5-28.5T183 559q11-11 28-11t28 11l143 143 339-339q11-11 28.5-11t28.5 11q11 11 11 28.5T778 420L410 788q-6 6-13 8.5t-15 2.5Z"></path></svg></button></pre></div>

<section id="analysis">
<h2>Analysis<a class="headerlink" href="#analysis" title="Link to this heading" x-intersect.margin.0%.0%.-70%.0%="activeSection = '#analysis'"><span>#</span></a></h2>
<p>Deltakit also provides tools for summarising the results of experiments. To calculate the LEP (Logical Error Probability) and its standard error, use the
<a class="reference internal" href="../_build/generated/deltakit.explorer.analysis.calculate_lep_and_lep_stddev.html#deltakit.explorer.analysis.calculate_lep_and_lep_stddev" title="deltakit.explorer.analysis.calculate_lep_and_lep_stddev"><code class="xref py py-meth docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">calculate_lep_and_lep_stddev</span></code></a> function.</p>
<div class="cell docutils container">
<div class="cell_input docutils container">
<div class="highlight-ipython3 notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><code><span id="line-1"><span class="kn">from</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">deltakit.explorer.analysis</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="n">calculate_lep_and_lep_stddev</span>
</span><span id="line-2"><span class="n">lep</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">lep_stddev</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">calculate_lep_and_lep_stddev</span><span class="p">(</span>
</span><span id="line-3">    <span class="n">fails</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">n_fails</span><span class="p">],</span>
</span><span id="line-4">    <span class="n">shots</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">n_shots</span><span class="p">],</span>
</span><span id="line-5"><span class="p">)</span>
</span><span id="line-6"><span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="sa">f</span><span class="s2">"LEP: </span><span class="si">{</span><span class="n">lep</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">0</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="si">}</span><span class="s2">, LEP std: </span><span class="si">{</span><span class="n">lep_stddev</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">0</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="si">}</span><span class="s2">"</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span><span id="line-7">
</span><span id="line-8"><span class="c1"># confirm the calculation manually</span>
</span><span id="line-9"><span class="kn">import</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">numpy</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="k">as</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">np</span>
</span><span id="line-10"><span class="kn">from</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">scipy</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="n">stats</span>
</span><span id="line-11"><span class="n">lep0</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">n_fails</span> <span class="o">/</span> <span class="n">n_shots</span>
</span><span id="line-12"><span class="n">lep_stddev0</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">stats</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">sem</span><span class="p">([</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">*</span><span class="n">n_fails</span> <span class="o">+</span> <span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">0</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">*</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">n_shots</span> <span class="o">-</span> <span class="n">n_fails</span><span class="p">),</span> <span class="n">ddof</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mi">0</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span><span id="line-13"><span class="n">np</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">testing</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">assert_allclose</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">lep</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">lep0</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span><span id="line-14"><span class="n">np</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">testing</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">assert_allclose</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">lep_stddev</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">lep_stddev0</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span></code><button class="copy z-20 inline-flex h-6 w-6 items-center justify-center rounded-md border border-border bg-background opacity-0 focus:opacity-100 text-sm font-medium transition-all hover:bg-muted absolute right-4 top-4 tooltipped tooltipped-n" data-tooltip="Copy code" type="button"><span class="sr-only">Copy code</span><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-100 absolute"><path d="M200 976q-33 0-56.5-23.5T120 896V376q0-17 11.5-28.5T160 336q17 0 28.5 11.5T200 376v520h400q17 0 28.5 11.5T640 936q0 17-11.5 28.5T600 976H200Zm160-160q-33 0-56.5-23.5T280 736V256q0-33 23.5-56.5T360 176h360q33 0 56.5 23.5T800 256v480q0 33-23.5 56.5T720 816H360Zm0-80h360V256H360v480Zm0 0V256v480Z"></path></svg><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-success-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-0 absolute"><path d="M382 799q-8 0-15-2.5t-13-8.5L182 616q-11-11-10.5-28.5T183 559q11-11 28-11t28 11l143 143 339-339q11-11 28.5-11t28.5 11q11 11 11 28.5T778 420L410 788q-6 6-13 8.5t-15 2.5Z"></path></svg></button></pre></div>

<div class="cell_output docutils container">
<div class="output stream highlight-myst-ansi notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><code><span id="line-1">LEP: 0.011, LEP std: 0.003298332912245215
</span></code><button class="copy z-20 inline-flex h-6 w-6 items-center justify-center rounded-md border border-border bg-background opacity-0 focus:opacity-100 text-sm font-medium transition-all hover:bg-muted absolute right-4 top-4 tooltipped tooltipped-n" data-tooltip="Copy code" type="button"><span class="sr-only">Copy code</span><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-100 absolute"><path d="M200 976q-33 0-56.5-23.5T120 896V376q0-17 11.5-28.5T160 336q17 0 28.5 11.5T200 376v520h400q17 0 28.5 11.5T640 936q0 17-11.5 28.5T600 976H200Zm160-160q-33 0-56.5-23.5T280 736V256q0-33 23.5-56.5T360 176h360q33 0 56.5 23.5T800 256v480q0 33-23.5 56.5T720 816H360Zm0-80h360V256H360v480Zm0 0V256v480Z"></path></svg><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-success-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-0 absolute"><path d="M382 799q-8 0-15-2.5t-13-8.5L182 616q-11-11-10.5-28.5T183 559q11-11 28-11t28 11l143 143 339-339q11-11 28.5-11t28.5 11q11 11 11 28.5T778 420L410 788q-6 6-13 8.5t-15 2.5Z"></path></svg></button></pre></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<p>Now you can start varying different parameters to see how that changes the logical error probability, such as the code distance. You can pass a list of decoder managers and number of shots to <a class="reference internal" href="../_build/generated/deltakit.decode.analysis.RunAllAnalysisEngine.html#deltakit.decode.analysis.RunAllAnalysisEngine" title="deltakit.decode.analysis.RunAllAnalysisEngine"><code class="xref py py-class docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">RunAllAnalysisEngine</span></code></a>, and use the <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">run</span></code> method to run your simulations. This method then returns a dataframe that summarises the results.</p>
<div class="cell docutils container">
<div class="cell_input docutils container">
<div class="highlight-ipython3 notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><code><span id="line-1"><span class="kn">from</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">deltakit.decode.analysis</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="n">RunAllAnalysisEngine</span>
</span><span id="line-2">
</span><span id="line-3"><span class="n">distances</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">5</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="mi">7</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="mi">9</span><span class="p">]</span>
</span><span id="line-4"><span class="n">all_shots</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">all_fails</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="p">[],</span> <span class="p">[]</span>
</span><span id="line-5"><span class="n">decoder_managers</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="p">[]</span>
</span><span id="line-6"><span class="k">for</span> <span class="n">d</span> <span class="ow">in</span> <span class="n">distances</span><span class="p">:</span>
</span><span id="line-7">    <span class="n">code</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">codes</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">RotatedPlanarCode</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">width</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="n">d</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">height</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="n">d</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span><span id="line-8">    <span class="n">circuit</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">css_code_memory_circuit</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">code</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">num_rounds</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="n">d</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">logical_basis</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="n">PauliBasis</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Z</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span><span id="line-9">    <span class="n">qpu</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">QPU</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">qubits</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="n">code</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">qubits</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">native_gates_and_times</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="n">native_gates</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">noise_model</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="n">noise_model</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span><span id="line-10">    <span class="n">qpu_circuit</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">qpu</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">compile_and_add_noise_to_circuit</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">circuit</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span><span id="line-11">    <span class="n">decoder</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">decoder_circuit</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">PyMatchingDecoder</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">construct_decoder_and_stim_circuit</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">qpu_circuit</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span><span id="line-12">    <span class="n">decoder_managers</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">append</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">StimDecoderManager</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">decoder_circuit</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">decoder</span><span class="p">))</span>
</span><span id="line-13">
</span><span id="line-14"><span class="n">engine</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">RunAllAnalysisEngine</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">"my experiment"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">decoder_managers</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="n">decoder_managers</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">max_shots</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mi">100000</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span><span id="line-15"><span class="n">df</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">engine</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">run</span><span class="p">()</span>
</span><span id="line-16"><span class="n">df</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'distance'</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">distances</span>
</span><span id="line-17"><span class="n">df</span>
</span></code><button class="copy z-20 inline-flex h-6 w-6 items-center justify-center rounded-md border border-border bg-background opacity-0 focus:opacity-100 text-sm font-medium transition-all hover:bg-muted absolute right-4 top-4 tooltipped tooltipped-n" data-tooltip="Copy code" type="button"><span class="sr-only">Copy code</span><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-100 absolute"><path d="M200 976q-33 0-56.5-23.5T120 896V376q0-17 11.5-28.5T160 336q17 0 28.5 11.5T200 376v520h400q17 0 28.5 11.5T640 936q0 17-11.5 28.5T600 976H200Zm160-160q-33 0-56.5-23.5T280 736V256q0-33 23.5-56.5T360 176h360q33 0 56.5 23.5T800 256v480q0 33-23.5 56.5T720 816H360Zm0-80h360V256H360v480Zm0 0V256v480Z"></path></svg><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-success-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-0 absolute"><path d="M382 799q-8 0-15-2.5t-13-8.5L182 616q-11-11-10.5-28.5T183 559q11-11 28-11t28 11l143 143 339-339q11-11 28.5-11t28.5 11q11 11 11 28.5T778 420L410 788q-6 6-13 8.5t-15 2.5Z"></path></svg></button></pre></div>

</div>

<div class="output stderr highlight-myst-ansi notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><code><span id="line-1">Evaluating codes:   0%|          | 0/3 [00:00<?, ?it/s]
</span></code><button class="copy z-20 inline-flex h-6 w-6 items-center justify-center rounded-md border border-border bg-background opacity-0 focus:opacity-100 text-sm font-medium transition-all hover:bg-muted absolute right-4 top-4 tooltipped tooltipped-n" data-tooltip="Copy code" type="button"><span class="sr-only">Copy code</span><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-100 absolute"><path d="M200 976q-33 0-56.5-23.5T120 896V376q0-17 11.5-28.5T160 336q17 0 28.5 11.5T200 376v520h400q17 0 28.5 11.5T640 936q0 17-11.5 28.5T600 976H200Zm160-160q-33 0-56.5-23.5T280 736V256q0-33 23.5-56.5T360 176h360q33 0 56.5 23.5T800 256v480q0 33-23.5 56.5T720 816H360Zm0-80h360V256H360v480Zm0 0V256v480Z"></path></svg><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" height="24" width="24" viewBox="0 96 960 960" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" class="copy-success-icon h-[14px] w-[14px] transition-all transform scale-0 absolute"><path d="M382 799q-8 0-15-2.5t-13-8.5L182 616q-11-11-10.5-28.5T183 559q11-11 28-11t28 11l143 143 339-339q11-11 28.5-11t28.5 11q11 11 11 28.5T778 420L410 788q-6 6-13 8.5t-15 2.5Z"></path></svg></button></pre></div>
